concepts.finding: FID (Frechet Inception Distance) compares feature distributions of real and fake images using Frechet distance between multivariate Gaussians in Inception feature space.
concepts.implication: Distribution-level comparison metrics (like FID) can evaluate search results holistically, comparing the distribution of retrieved items to the distribution of relevant items.
concepts.open_question: Can a FID-like distribution comparison metric improve search evaluation beyond pointwise relevance metrics?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: A distribution-based search evaluation metric will better detect ranking degradation than pointwise NDCG alone.

concepts.finding: Progressive Growing of GANs (ProGAN) starts with low-resolution images and progressively increases resolution, leading to faster and more stable training.
concepts.implication: Progressive training (starting simple, gradually increasing complexity) can improve training stability and speed, applicable to training search models by starting with easy queries and progressing to hard ones.
concepts.open_question: Does curriculum learning (easy to hard queries) improve final model quality for search ranking?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: A curriculum training strategy starting with high-CTR queries and progressing to tail queries will produce a ranking model with 3%+ higher NDCG than standard training order.
